resources.string:
Vic Camera Alerts mostly runs inside Android Auto, not on this phone screen.

To use it:
1. Grant location + notification access below.
2. Plug your phone into Android Auto (or use wireless Android Auto).
3. In the Android Auto app on your phone, go to Settings → tap the version number 10 times to unlock Developer settings → enable “Unknown sources”.
4. Open Android Auto in the car — “Vic Camera Alerts” will appear as an app icon showing nearby approved mobile camera locations, and it will automatically start sending a heads-up notification when you get close to one.

Alerts are straight-line proximity, not route-aware — you may get a heads-up for a camera that's close by on a different road. You can also start/stop alerts on the phone directly below, without Android Auto connected, to test them.

Camera location data comes from the Victorian Government’s monthly published list. The app checks for updates once a day over the internet and falls back to the copy bundled at install time when offline.
Notifies you when you\'re near an approved mobile camera location.
Vic Camera Alerts is checking your location for nearby cameras.
